sources: Technical Analysis Using Multiple Timeframes by Brian Shannon
Pring on Price Patterns by Martin Pring
metadata.description: ULTIMATE JSON Rulebook with Pring Indicators (RSI, MFI, KST, ADX)

stage_1_accumulation.math: Price crossing moving averages; 200 SMA flattening. Moving averages (10, 20, 50) begin to cross above and below each other. Contraction of price ranges.
stage_1_accumulation.psychology: Boredom, skepticism, numbness, complacency, and denial. Gradual shift in control from sellers to a neutral environment. Institutional quiet accumulation.
stage_1_accumulation.action: Avoid trend trading. Monitor for higher lows, increased volume, and frequent tests of resistance near the end of the stage for AVWAP/resistance breakout.

stage_2_markup.math: Price > 10 SMA > 20 SMA > 50 SMA > 200 SMA. All SMAs have positive slope. Higher highs and higher lows.
stage_2_markup.psychology: Greed, thrill, belief, optimism, and fear of missing out. Short sellers trapped (fear). Trend becomes obvious and participation accelerates.
stage_2_markup.action: Aggressively scale positions via Fractional Kelly. Buy on strength or shallow pullbacks. Lock in partial profits on signs of tiring.

stage_3_distribution.math: Price breaks short-term SMAs; 20/50 SMA crossing. 200 SMA flattening. High volatility and price contraction.
stage_3_distribution.psychology: Euphoria transitioning to anxiety and denial. Institutional unloading to retail. Buyers lose conviction as momentum stalls.
stage_3_distribution.action: Lock profits. Veto new long positions. Monitor for trend confusion and downward SMA crossovers.

stage_4_decline.math: Price < 10 SMA < 20 SMA < 50 SMA < 200 SMA. 200 SMA slope < 0. Lower highs and lower lows.
stage_4_decline.psychology: Panic, capitulation, fear, depression, and despair. Sellers aggressively control the trend.
stage_4_decline.action: Maintain cash or aggressively initiate short setups.

rectangle_consolidation.math: Volume contractions as patterns form; breakout points use pattern depth for projection.
rectangle_consolidation.action: Await clear breakout direction before committing; use stop-loss to protect against whipsaws.

head_and_shoulders_top.math: Neckline breach confirms reversal; slower volume during right shoulder.
head_and_shoulders_top.action: Prepare for short positions upon confirmation; watch for rally attempts after neckline breach as potential short opportunities.

rectangle.structure: Volume trends shrink; breakout direction implies move; depth projects move.
rectangle.psychology: Price history shows a balance between buyers and sellers leading to sharp moves.
vision_failure_markers: Shrinking volume on upside breakout implies potential whipsaw.
No expansion in volume during breakout.
rectangle.institutional_footprint: Institutions accumulate positions within the range before breakout

symmetrical_triangle.structure: Converging higher lows and lower highs indicating a price coil. Touched multiple times.
symmetrical_triangle.psychology: Diminishing volatility leading to a dramatic imbalance between buyers and sellers. Fine balance before an explosive move.
symmetrical_triangle.volume_confirmation: Progressive volume contraction into the apex. Explosion of volume on breakout.
vision_failure_markers: Breakout occurring too deep into apex (>75%)
Lack of volume on thrust
Whipsaw fakeouts
symmetrical_triangle.institutional_footprint: Institutions are moving to resolve the battle between buyers and sellers, usually indicated by volume expansion upon breakout

ascending_triangle.structure: Flat upper resistance with rising lower support line.
ascending_triangle.psychology: Buyers are increasingly aggressive, willing to buy at higher prices, while sellers hold firm at a specific resistance level.
ascending_triangle.volume_confirmation: Drying up on pullbacks, expanding on advances toward resistance. Strong volume on breakout.
vision_failure_markers: Spring/Upthrust failing to hold breakout level
Low volume on breakout
ascending_triangle.institutional_footprint: Institutional interest builds as the price consistently tests resistance

descending_triangle.structure: Flat lower support with a declining upper resistance line.
descending_triangle.psychology: Sellers are increasingly aggressive, willing to sell at lower prices, while buyers hold firm at a specific support level.
descending_triangle.volume_confirmation: Volume expands on declines toward support and shrinks on rallies.
vision_failure_markers: Bear trap failing to hold breakdown level
Unusual volume expansion on upside retracements

head_and_shoulders_top.structure: Final rally (Head) flanked by two smaller rallies (Shoulders) on a common neckline.
head_and_shoulders_top.psychology: Exhaustion of buying power. Transition from advancing peaks to declining peaks. Distribution from strong to weak hands.
head_and_shoulders_top.volume_confirmation: Heaviest volume on the left shoulder, lighter on head, lightest on right shoulder. Heavy volume on neckline break.
vision_failure_markers: Neckline break fails to hold (Bear Trap)
Right shoulder shakeout (falsely breaks above resistance)
Expanding volume on right shoulder rally
head_and_shoulders_top.institutional_footprint: The absence of volume suggests no institutional support for the breakout.

reverse_head_and_shoulders_bottom.structure: Final decline (Head) flanked by two smaller declines (Shoulders) on a common neckline.
reverse_head_and_shoulders_bottom.psychology: Exhaustion of selling pressure. Transition from declining troughs to rising troughs. Accumulation by strong hands.
reverse_head_and_shoulders_bottom.volume_confirmation: Heavy volume on left shoulder/head, contracting on right shoulder decline, huge expansion on upside breakout.
vision_failure_markers: Neckline break fails to hold (Bull Trap)
Lack of volume explosion on upside breakout
reverse_head_and_shoulders_bottom.institutional_footprint: Accumulation phase where informed investors are accumulating in anticipation of rising prices

double_top_and_bottom.structure: Two peaks at roughly the same price level (Top) or two troughs at the same level (Bottom).
double_top_and_bottom.psychology: Failure to push past a previously established extreme. Exhaustion and ultimate reversal of trend.
double_top_and_bottom.volume_confirmation: Volume should be noticeably lighter on the second peak (or trough) and expand drastically on the reversal breakout.
vision_failure_markers: Volume is heavier on the second peak
Failure to break the intervening trough/peak
double_top_and_bottom.institutional_footprint: Smart money accumulates at lower prices, evident in volume pattern

broadening_formation.structure: Expanding price range with higher highs and lower lows (reverse of a triangle).
broadening_formation.psychology: Highly emotional, out-of-control market. Lack of sponsorship from smart money. Extreme volatility.
broadening_formation.volume_confirmation: Irregular and expanding volume as the pattern widens.
vision_failure_markers: Premature breakouts
Trendless volume behavior

gaps.structure: A blank space on the chart where no trading occurred.
gaps.psychology: Emotional reaction to news or panic/euphoria. Left behind spaces represent dynamic support/resistance.
gaps.volume_confirmation: High volume on Breakaway Gaps; climax volume on Exhaustion Gaps.
vision_failure_markers: Gap is quickly filled (closed), negating the momentum
gaps.institutional_footprint: Institutional buying typically follows a breakaway gap, indicating strong interest and conviction.

outside_and_inside_bars.structure: Outside bar fully engulfs the prior bar's range. Inside bar is completely contained within the prior bar's range.
outside_and_inside_bars.psychology: Outside bar shows a dramatic battle and reversal of control. Inside bar shows extreme equilibrium and indecision.
outside_and_inside_bars.volume_confirmation: High volume on an outside reversal bar confirms the trend change.
vision_failure_markers: Lack of volume on outside bar
Inside bar breakout failing to follow through

pinocchio_bars_and_key_reversals.structure: Long wicks breaking a support/resistance level but closing back within the range.
pinocchio_bars_and_key_reversals.psychology: A 'lie' or trap. The market rejects the extreme price level, shaking out weak hands.
pinocchio_bars_and_key_reversals.volume_confirmation: Climax volume on the extreme wick, showing absorption by smart money.
vision_failure_markers: Bar fails to close significantly back inside the range
Low volume at the extreme wick

head_and_shoulders.structure: Volume higher on left shoulder, declines on right shoulder; neckline violation confirms pattern.
head_and_shoulders.psychology: Market psychology shifts from bullish to bearish, signaling trend reversal.
vision_failure_markers: Lack of clear inbound uptrend preceding the pattern.
Volume characteristics not aligning with typical H&S formations.

rectangle_reversal_pattern.structure: Identify support and resistance levels with horizontal trendlines; monitor break below support for downside reversal signal or break above for upside reversal.
rectangle_reversal_pattern.psychology: Transitional phase where optimism and pessimism balance out before one gains upper hand.
vision_failure_markers: Failure to break out decisively from the trading range
False breakouts with insufficient volume

rectangle_continuation_pattern.structure: Monitor upper resistance breakout for uptrend continuation; count the number of touches on boundaries to assess significance.
rectangle_continuation_pattern.psychology: Buyers take control post-consolidation, optimism in prevailing trend continues.
vision_failure_markers: Break fails to hold above previous resistance
Entering new range without directional flow confirmation

accumulation_formation.structure: Identify bottoming phase with support level that fails to break lower; observe for decisive break above resistance to confirm upward trend reversal.
accumulation_formation.psychology: Informed buyers accumulating from pessimistic sellers amidst bad news, betting on future improvements.
vision_failure_markers: Protracted low volume during consolidation
Lack of bullish follow-through post-breakout

distribution_formation.structure: Identify topping phase with resistance level repeatedly tested; look for break below support to confirm downward trend reversal.
distribution_formation.psychology: Informed sellers distributing to uninformed buyers amid good news, signaling market top.
vision_failure_markers: Sustained buying interest at support levels
Resilient upward spikes on high volume

always_in.structure: Spike in direction of the trend required for confidence
vision_failure_markers: Lack of directional conviction
Contrary large moves

barbwire.structure: Three or more overlapping bars with tails
barbwire.psychology: Indecision and confusion in the market
vision_failure_markers: Trend breakout
Sharp directional move

breakout_bar.structure: Strong trend bar creating new highs/lows
breakout_bar.psychology: Market confidence in new price levels
vision_failure_markers: Immediate reversal
Weak follow-through

breakout_pullback.structure: Pullback within a few bars after breakout
vision_failure_markers: Extended consolidation
Breakout failure

double_bottom.structure: Low of current bar matches earlier swing low
double_bottom.psychology: Support confluence and bullish reversal anticipation
vision_failure_markers: Failure to hold support
Strong downtrend override
double_bottom.institutional_footprint: Smart money accumulates at lower prices, evident in volume pattern

double_top.structure: High of current bar matches earlier swing high
double_top.psychology: Resistance confluence and bearish reversal anticipation
vision_failure_markers: Failure to hold resistance
Strong uptrend override

climax.structure: Rapid move followed by sharp reversal
climax.psychology: Exhaustion of trend followed by reversion
vision_failure_markers: Trend continuation
Consolidation instead of reversal

ascending_broadening_wedge.structure: Rising highs and lows, but range is widening. Highly unstable.
ascending_broadening_wedge.psychology: Extreme emotional exhaustion. Lack of institutional control.
ascending_broadening_wedge.action: Veto all long positions. High risk of a violent reversal.

fan_lines.structure: Three trendlines drawn from a peak at different angles.
fan_lines.rule: Violation of the third fan line confirms a major trend reversal.
fan_lines.action: Exit all positions and wait for new Stage 1 consolidation.

flags_and_pennants.structure: Brief consolidation after a sharp pole. Volume dries up during flag.
flags_and_pennants.measure_rule: Target = Breakout point + Length of the flagpole.
vision_failure_markers: Flag lasting longer than 4 weeks (becomes a rectangle)
Rising volume during consolidation

wedges.rising_wedge: Converging rising lines. Extremely bearish in an uptrend (exhaustion).
wedges.falling_wedge: Converging falling lines. Extremely bullish in a downtrend (accumulation).
wedges.rule: Must see a volume dry-up into the apex.

diamond_formation.structure: A broadening formation followed by a symmetrical triangle. Rare but highly potent.
diamond_formation.psychology: Extreme emotional expansion followed by sudden institutional clamping. Usually a major top.
diamond_formation.action: Wait for the break of the diamond support. Target is the height of the diamond.

saucer_bottom.structure: A long, slow rounding accumulation. Price and volume both form a bowl shape.
saucer_bottom.psychology: Gradual exhaustion of sellers followed by stealth institutional vacuuming.
saucer_bottom.action: Buy on the break of the rim with a massive volume expansion.

spike_reversal.structure: A sharp, parabolic move followed by an immediate reversal on climax volume.
spike_reversal.rule: Usually occurs after a long Stage 2 run. High risk of a Stage 4 collapse.

inverse_saucer.structure: A rounding top. Slow, quiet distribution.
inverse_saucer.psychology: Buying interest slowly evaporates. Smart money exits silently.

coppock_curve.logic: Long-term momentum oscillator for identifying bull market starts.
coppock_curve.signal: When the curve turns up from below 0 after a major decline.

warning_signs: Low volume on breakout
Immediate return below resistance level
false_breakout.rejection_logic: Lack of true institutional buying, indicative of retail-driven price action

warning_signs: Reversal after penetration of neckline
Violating a previously valid trendline
failed_head_and_shoulders.rejection_logic: Indicates a market reversal or failure of the anticipated trend, resulting in a sharp move in the opposite direction

warning_signs: Low volume on breakout
Retracement of 50% or more of the rally from the second low to the breakout point
failed_double_bottom.rejection_logic: Lack of buying pressure and momentum to sustain upward movement, indicating vulnerability to reversal

warning_signs: Volume fails to expand on breakout
Price struggles to move beyond previous highs/lows
shrinking_volume_breakout_in_a_triangle.rejection_logic: Lack of commitment from big money players on the breakout, increasing risk of false signal

warning_signs: Lack of volume expansion on breakout
Formation morphs into another pattern (e.g., head-and-shoulders)
failed_symmetrical_triangle.rejection_logic: The absence of volume suggests no institutional support for the breakout.

warning_signs: Weak volume on breakout
False break past horizontal line followed by vigorous move in opposite direction
failed_right-angled_triangle.rejection_logic: Reflects false continuation signal, often reversed by large position unwinding.

warning_signs: Gap occurs after a long price advance
Volume very high but price fails to advance further
exhaustion_gap.rejection_logic: Gap is caused by retail traders, while institutions are likely selling into strength

warning_signs: Gap up followed by a gap down creating an isolated price range
Accompanied by high volume
island_reversal.rejection_logic: Island reversals reflect an abrupt change in sentiment, usually resolving in the opposite direction.

warning_signs: Price temporarily breaks a significant level but closes back below/above it
Long wick above/below
pinocchio_bar.rejection_logic: Signals a false breakout, typically a sucker move to trap uninformed traders into taking the wrong position.

warning_signs: Breakout against the primary trend
RSI near oversold/overbought
contra-trend_breakout.rejection_logic: High likelihood of being a whipsaw due to dominant primary trend resuming

warning_signs: Price breaks resistance but oscillator shows negative divergence
Lack of volume expansion
divergence_and_breakout.rejection_logic: Potential momentum loss implied by divergence, indicating false breakout

warning_signs: Breakout with no surge in volume
Overextended price from 200-day SMA
bull_trap.rejection_logic: Unsustainable breakout subject to quick reversal, institutions not supporting the move

warning_signs: Heavy price rejection at previous support levels now resistance
Weakening RSI below 50
overhead_supply_resistance.rejection_logic: Institutional sellers stymie upward momentum, high risk of reversion

warning_signs: Exponential price move followed by high volume sell-off
Relative Strength drops sharply
climactic_top.rejection_logic: Distribution phase not recognized promptly, indicating a shift to bearish control

warning_signs: Initial breakout followed by bearish engulfing candle
RSI divergence
head-fake.rejection_logic: Institutional selling or short positions drive the immediate pullback

warning_signs: Price closes below breakout level next day
Volume decreases
failed_breakout.rejection_logic: Institutions may lack confidence, leading to a quick reversal and potential for deep pullbacks.

warning_signs: Rapid price retreat post initial breakout
Lack of follow-through volume
false_start.rejection_logic: Indicates lack of institutional support; breakout lacks underlying demand.

warning_signs: Price breaks below support but quickly rebounds
Significant volume spike
bear_trap.rejection_logic: Traders are tricked into selling at a perceived breakdown, but institutions accumulate shares at discounted prices.

vision_agent_directives.skeptical_auditor_prompt: Reject assets trading in the middle of a consolidation range. Only approve charts where the current daily candle has decisively broken structural resistance on visually expanding volume, or is emerging from a tightly coiled bull flag.
vision_agent_directives.avwap_validation: Ensure current price is respecting the AVWAP anchored to the most recent earnings gap or multi-month low.
vision_agent_directives.false_breakout_checks: Check if price falls back into the pattern violating a minor low (for longs) or minor high (for shorts). Verify if breakout lacks required volume expansion.

rsi_divergence.bullish: Price makes lower low while RSI makes higher low. Indicates selling exhaustion.
rsi_divergence.bearish: Price makes higher high while RSI makes lower high. Indicates buying exhaustion.
rsi_divergence.confirmation: Wait for RSI to cross above 50 for bullish entry.

money_flow_index_mfi.institutional_accumulation: MFI rising above 60 while price remains flat in a coil.
money_flow_index_mfi.institutional_distribution: MFI dropping below 40 while price is still near highs.
money_flow_index_mfi.role: Detects volume-weighted momentum before price moves.

adx_trend_strength.stage_2_filter: ADX must be > 20 and rising. This prevents buying in choppy, trendless ranges.
adx_trend_strength.exhaustion: ADX > 50 often signals a trend is nearing a climax/exhaustion point.

know_sure_thing_kst.formula: Weighted average of 4 smoothed ROC timeframes.
know_sure_thing_kst.signal: KST crossing above its 9-day signal line while in an Incubator is a High-Conviction Ignition.

bollinger_squeeze.action: Wait for the break of the upper band with Volume ROC expansion.

stochastics.oversold_buy: Buy when %K crosses above %D below 20 in a Stage 2 uptrend.
stochastics.overbought_sell: Exit or hedge when %K crosses below %D above 80.
stochastics.divergence: Price makes higher high, Stochastics makes lower high (Bearish Warning).

macd_histogram.momentum_lead: Histogram turning up from below zero before price breakout.
macd_histogram.exhaustion: Histogram flattening at extreme highs suggests the move is over-extended.

volume_breadth.advance_decline: Market is strong if more stocks are rising than falling (Breadth Confirmation).
volume_breadth.volume_confirmation: Expansion on up days, contraction on down days (Institutional DNA).

pring_market_cycles.the_4_year_cycle: The standard stock market cycle typically lasts 4 years, driven by the interest rate cycle.

economic_stages.stage_1: Bonds turn up, stocks bottom. Buy Rate-Sensitive (Utilities/Banks).
economic_stages.stage_2: Stocks turn up, economy recovers. Buy Growth/Momentum (Tech/Retail).
economic_stages.stage_4: Bonds turn down. Risk of topping.
economic_stages.stage_5: Stocks turn down. Defensive shift (Consumer Staples).
economic_stages.stage_6: Commodities turn down. Recession mode. Cash is king.

pring_breadth_indicators.advance_decline_line: If A/D line is making new highs but price is not, the market is strong. If price makes new highs but A/D is flat, it is a divergence (DANGER).
pring_breadth_indicators.nh_nl_ratio: New Highs minus New Lows. A spike in NH indicates a Breadth Thrust. A spike in NL indicates capitulation.
pring_breadth_indicators.mccllellan_oscillator: Measures the speed of breadth. Crossing above 0 is an Institutional Buy signal.
pring_breadth_indicators.breadth_thrust: A move from < 40% to > 60% in 10 days indicates a massive institutional rotation.

pring_intermarket_rules.bond_equity_relationship: Bonds usually lead stocks at major turns. If Bonds (TLT) are crashing, stocks will eventually follow.
pring_intermarket_rules.gold_inflation_proxy: Gold prices lead inflation-sensitive stocks. Watch for Gold breakouts to confirm Energy/Materials trades.
pring_intermarket_rules.dollar_momentum: Strong Dollar is usually bearish for Emerging Markets and Commodities.

pring_sentiment_rules.put_call_ratio: Extreme high (>1.2) indicates excessive fear (Bottoming). Extreme low (<0.6) indicates excessive greed (Topping).
pring_sentiment_rules.advisor_sentiment: When 70%+ of advisors are bullish, the market is vulnerable to a Stage 3 distribution.

pring_price_nuances.the_test: Price returns to a breakout level on low volume. If it holds, it confirms institutional support.
pring_price_nuances.the_throwback: Similar to the test but more aggressive. Occurs in 70% of valid breakouts.
pring_price_nuances.volume_v: A V-shaped volume surge at a bottom indicates an institutional cleaning out of weak hands.

pring_gap_analysis.breakaway_gap: Occurs at the start of a move. High volume. Should NOT be filled quickly.
pring_gap_analysis.runaway_gap: Occurs in the middle of a move. Confirms trend strength. Acts as support.
pring_gap_analysis.exhaustion_gap: Occurs at the end of a long move. Climax volume. Filled quickly (Island Reversal risk).

pring_candlestick_filters.the_hammer: Bullish reversal. Long lower wick at support. Confirms institutional absorption.
pring_candlestick_filters.the_shooting_star: Bearish reversal. Long upper wick at resistance. Confirms institutional distribution.
pring_candlestick_filters.the_doji: Indecision. A doji at a breakout point requires a second-day confirmation.

pring_volatility_rules.atr_expansion: A breakout is only valid if ATR(3) > ATR(20) at the moment of breach.
pring_volatility_rules.bollinger_bandwidth: Buy only when bandwidth is at its lowest point of the last 60 days (The Pinch).

pring_intermarket_ratios.copper_gold_ratio: Rising ratio indicates global growth (Bullish for Equities). Falling ratio indicates deflation (Bearish).
pring_intermarket_ratios.transports_vs_industrials: Dow Theory rule. Transports must confirm Industrials for a valid bull market.

pring_momentum_waves.one_two_three_reversal: 1. Trendline break, 2. Test of the high, 3. Break of the intervening low.
pring_momentum_waves.rule: This is the most reliable way to identify a Stage 2 to Stage 3 transition.

pring_ma_ribbons.structure: A cluster of 10+ EMAs (e.g., 3 to 100 periods).
pring_ma_ribbons.fanning_out: Indicates strong, healthy momentum in Stage 2.
pring_ma_ribbons.converging: Indicates trend exhaustion and risk of reversal.

pring_point_and_figure.horizontal_count: Measuring the width of a consolidation to project the height of the move.
pring_point_and_figure.vertical_count: Measuring the length of a breakout thrust to project the next resistance.

pring_volume_advanced.upside_breakout_confirmation: Volume should increase by at least 50% relative to the 10-day average during the breakout bar.
pring_volume_advanced.downside_breakout_confirmation: Volume does NOT need to increase for a valid downside break, but heavy volume increases the probability of a crash.
pring_volume_advanced.exhaustion_blowoff: Price acceleration on 5x average volume signals a climax.
pring_volume_advanced.volume_at_the_top: High volume without price progress (Churning) indicates distribution.
pring_volume_advanced.volume_at_the_bottom: Volume typically hits a multi-month low before the saucer rim begins to rise.

pring_sentiment_matrix.mutual_fund_cash_ratio: High cash levels (>10%) indicate institutional fear (Bullish). Low cash levels (<4%) indicate euphoria (Bearish).
pring_sentiment_matrix.odd_lot_short_sales: Measures small-trader sentiment. High odd-lot shorting indicates a bottom is near.
pring_sentiment_matrix.member_short_ratio: Measures NYSE specialists. If they are shorting heavily, the top is close.

pring_cycle_indicators.the_decennial_cycle: The tendency for markets to bottom in years ending in 3 or 7.
pring_cycle_indicators.the_election_cycle: Year 3 (Pre-election) is historically the strongest year for equities.
pring_cycle_indicators.the_presidential_cycle: Year 1 and 2 are typically corrective or sideways.

pring_trendline_rules.the_fan_principle: After three trendline violations, a major trend change is 95% certain.
pring_trendline_rules.the_acceleration_rule: When a trendline gets steeper (e.g., 30 deg to 45 deg to 60 deg), the climax is approaching.
pring_trendline_rules.the_internal_trendline: A line that cuts through the price data, connecting as many internal peaks/troughs as possible.

pring_comprehensive_library.ch_1_definition_of_trend: A trend is a series of higher highs and higher lows (Bullish) or lower highs and lower lows (Bearish). Break of this sequence is the FIRST warning.

ch_3_support_and_resistance.rule_2: The more times a level is tested, the weaker it becomes (Institutional absorption).
ch_3_support_and_resistance.rule_3: Round numbers (100, 500, 1000) act as psychological anchors.

ch_6_momentum_principles.oscillator_rule_1: Momentum always leads price at market turns.
ch_6_momentum_principles.oscillator_rule_2: Divergence is more important than the absolute value of the indicator.

ch_10_point_and_figure.triple_top_buy: A breakout above two previous peaks. Highly reliable.
ch_10_point_and_figure.catapult_formation: A breakout followed by a pullback that holds above the breakout point.

pring_comprehensive_library.ch_14_interest_rates: Rising rates are a headwind for growth stocks; falling rates are a tailwind for banks/real estate.

ch_24_breadth_thrusts.zweig_thrust: Occurs when the 10-day moving average of Advances / (Advances + Declines) moves from < 0.40 to > 0.615.
ch_24_breadth_thrusts.implication: Signals a massive bull market start (High conviction).

pring_extended_patterns.broadening_top: Expanding range after a long advance. High emotional instability.
pring_extended_patterns.complex_head_and_shoulders: Pattern with multiple heads or multiple shoulders. Stronger reversal potential.
pring_extended_patterns.triple_top: Three distinct peaks at the same level. Institutional refusal to pay higher prices.
pring_extended_patterns.triple_bottom: Three distinct troughs. Institutional refusal to sell lower.
pring_extended_patterns.bump_and_run: Sharp angle increase followed by a crash back to the original trendline.

pring_indicator_math.mfi_math: Typical Price = (High + Low + Close) / 3. Raw Money Flow = Typical Price * Volume.
